topStories.headline: 'Chonkers,' a 2,000-Pound Steller Sea Lion, Draws National Attention to Pier 39
topStories.body:
A massive Steller sea lion — nicknamed 'Chonkers' by the online community after a Pier 39 Instagram post went viral in mid-March — has been drawing an unusual surge of visitors to the pier's K-Dock marine area. The animal, estimated at 2,000 to 2,500 pounds, is roughly a third larger than the California sea lions that have colonized the docks since the late 1980s.

The Pier 39 post was picked up by the Wall Street Journal and dozens of international outlets in the weeks since, bringing visitors from across the Bay Area and beyond specifically to catch a glimpse of the outsized animal lounging among its considerably smaller neighbors.

Steller sea lions (Eumetopias jubatus) are the largest of the eared seals and are less commonly seen in the Bay than their California counterparts. The Marine Mammal Center, which monitors the local sea lion population, noted that individual Steller sea lions do sometimes visit the K-Dock but rarely for extended stays.

Pier 39 staff ask visitors to keep a respectful distance and observe posted viewing guidelines. The sea lion area is visible from the free public observation deck at no charge.
topStories.imageQuery: sea lion pier San Francisco dock

topStories.headline: Wharf Records One Million March Visitors — and the World's Top 'Tourist Trap' Title, Again
topStories.body:
Fisherman's Wharf recorded its first one-million-visitor March since the pandemic — a 24 percent increase over March 2024, according to the Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District. The milestone arrived alongside an unwelcome honor: a travel data analysis by eSIM provider Nomad ranked Fisherman's Wharf the world's number-one 'tourist trap' for the second consecutive year, based on aggregated online reviews.

District representatives pointed to $1.4 million in recent Promenade improvements and a wave of new businesses along the waterfront as evidence of meaningful evolution. The Port of San Francisco's larger Fisherman's Wharf Forward project — a roughly $10 million public-private renovation — is on schedule for a summer 2026 completion.

A new public plaza is under construction on Taylor Street on the site of the former Alioto's Restaurant, which was demolished in January. The design includes public seating with views of the Golden Gate Bridge, space for outdoor retail or beverage service, windscreens, and landscaping. A new inner lagoon overlook along Al Scoma Way and new lighting celebrating the fishing fleet are also planned for summer completion.
topStories.sourceName: KION / CBS News SF / Port of San Francisco
topStories.imageQuery: Fisherman's Wharf San Francisco waterfront promenade

topStories.headline: Demolition of Fire-Damaged Verdi Building on Union Street Halted by Tenants Appeal
topStories.body:
Demolition of the Verdi Club building at 659 Union Street — vacant since a 2018 fire left it structurally compromised — has been halted after the North Beach Tenants Committee filed an appeal of the emergency demolition permit obtained by developer Red Bridge.

The committee argues the 2023 permit described work limited to a 50-foot rear wall section, not a full teardown, and that the building may be eligible for the National Register of Historic Places — a threshold that would require a full environmental review before demolition could proceed. The committee also alleges that demolition would circumvent prior commitments to rehouse residents displaced by the 2018 fire.

Red Bridge and city agencies have characterized the 'fifty-foot section' language as a clerical error and maintain the full structure poses a public safety risk. Separate partial demolition permits have been filed with the city, according to SF YIMBY.

The appeal is pending a hearing date. The building, a neighborhood landmark on the corner of Union and Powell, has been supported by temporary shoring for eight years.
topStories.sourceName: SF Standard / SF YIMBY / ABC7 News
topStories.imageQuery: North Beach San Francisco street Victorian building historic

events.title: Tuesdays at North Beach: Poetry Reading Series
events.description: Weekly poetry readings at the North Beach Branch Library. Open to all; no registration required. Part of an ongoing series hosted in partnership with the Friends of the San Francisco Public Library.
events.location: North Beach Branch Library, 850 Columbus Ave.

events.title: Off the Grid Food Trucks at Levi's Plaza
events.description: Rotating food truck market in the sunken plaza, with regular appearances by Curry Up Now, Cousins Maine Lobster, and others. Outdoor seating in the plaza with Bay Area sunshine (fog permitting).

events.title: 'Chonkers' the Steller Sea Lion at Pier 39 K-Dock
events.description: The unusually large Steller sea lion nicknamed 'Chonkers' continues to draw visitors to the K-Dock sea lion viewing area. Free to view from the public observation deck. Pier 39 asks visitors to observe posted distance guidelines.

events.title: Aquarium of the Bay: Special Programs Day
events.description: The Aquarium of the Bay at Pier 39 hosts rotating special programs for families and adults. Check the aquarium's website for current featured experiences and timed-entry ticket availability.

events.title: Coit Tower Open — Pioneer Park & WPA Murals
events.description: Pioneer Park and the ground-floor WPA murals painted by 26 artists in 1934 are free and open daily. The observation deck at the summit is accessible by elevator (fee applies). The Coit Tower parking lot and Telegraph Hill Boulevard repaving, completed in 2025, are fully open.
events.price: Ground floor & murals: free. Elevator to top: fee applies.

events.title: Fisherman's Wharf Forward — Taylor St. Plaza Under Construction
events.description: The Port of San Francisco's new public plaza on Taylor Street — on the footprint of the former Alioto's Restaurant — is under active construction, with a summer 2026 target. The surrounding Wharf Promenade remains open during construction.
events.location: Taylor St. at Jefferson St., Fisherman's Wharf

events.title: Public Survey: Embarcadero Plaza & Sue Bierman Park Redesign
events.description: SF Rec & Parks and partners seek input on a $40 million redesign merging Embarcadero Plaza and Sue Bierman Park into a single 5-acre waterfront park. A community meeting follows on April 28. Survey available at sfrecpark.org.

events.title: Community Meeting: Embarcadero Plaza / Sue Bierman Park
events.description: Third public design meeting for the $40 million renovation that would replace the Vaillancourt Fountain with green space and recreational features. Venue TBD — verify at sfrecpark.org before attending.

moreNews.headline: Grocery Outlet Build-Out Underway at Former North Beach Safeway Site
moreNews.body: Construction is underway at the former Safeway space at NorthPoint Centre near Fisherman's Wharf, where Grocery Outlet Bargain Market is building out a roughly 30,000-square-foot store. The Safeway closed in May 2023, leaving thousands of North Beach, Russian Hill, Telegraph Hill, and Fisherman's Wharf residents without a full-service grocery within easy walking distance. A 15-year lease was signed in January 2025. The opening, initially projected for early 2026, is running slightly behind schedule according to Hoodline's reporting. The investment is approximately $5 million and is expected to create more than 50 jobs.
moreNews.imageQuery: grocery store construction retail San Francisco neighborhood

moreNews.headline: Private Fireworks Near Pier 27 Surprises Neighborhood — Second Year Running
moreNews.body: A private fireworks display launched from near Pier 27 on the Embarcadero around 9:45 PM on Saturday caught North Beach and waterfront residents off guard for the second consecutive year. The roughly 10-to-12-minute show was staged by Walnut Creek-based Computers and Structures, Inc. for a company anniversary celebration and had been approved by the Port of San Francisco. The city chose not to publicize the event in advance to avoid drawing large crowds requiring additional police resources. Dog owners in North Beach and along the northern Embarcadero were particularly affected. The Port has indicated it is reviewing its approach to advance notice for approved private fireworks events.
moreNews.imageQuery: San Francisco Bay fireworks night waterfront

moreNews.headline: Qua O La Opens Croatian-Italian Wine Bar on Grant Avenue
moreNews.body: A new wine bar at 1318 Grant Ave. is bringing what its owners describe as the first Croatian-inflected menu in San Francisco since the longtime Dalmatian restaurant Albona closed in 2018. Qua O La offers ćevapi (grilled minced meat), burek (flaky pastry), and a wine list emphasizing Croatian and Italian producers alongside familiar Italian selections. The opening adds to a string of new food and beverage concepts along Grant Avenue's North Beach restaurant corridor in recent months.
moreNews.imageQuery: wine bar intimate interior candles San Francisco

restaurants.name: Caffè Trieste
restaurants.description: San Francisco's first espresso bar, open since 1956. Famous for Saturday accordion performances. Celebrating its 70th anniversary in 2026.

restaurants.name: Tony's Pizza Napoletana
restaurants.description: World champion Neapolitan pizza with multiple specialty wood-burning ovens. Reservations strongly recommended for dinner.

restaurants.name: Sotto Mare
restaurants.description: Classic cioppino and local seafood in a no-frills room. A neighborhood institution. Cash preferred at lunch.

restaurants.name: Vesuvio Café
restaurants.description: Iconic Beat-era bar across the alley from City Lights. Full bar; light snacks.

restaurants.name: North Beach Restaurant
restaurants.description: Long-running neighborhood Italian, recently renovated. Classic pasta, seafood, and Tuscan-style entrées.

restaurants.name: Qua O La
restaurants.description: New Croatian-Italian wine bar on Grant Avenue. Features ćevapi, burek, and Croatian and Italian wines. Opened April 2026.

restaurants.name: Scoma's Restaurant
restaurants.description: A Fisherman's Wharf institution since 1965. Fresh Dungeness crab, sand dabs, Bay views. Valet parking available.

restaurants.name: Fog Harbor Fish House
restaurants.description: Sustainably sourced seafood with wide Bay views. Full bar; outdoor deck.

restaurants.name: Boudin Bakery & Café
restaurants.description: San Francisco sourdough since 1849. Clam chowder in sourdough bread bowls. On-site museum and open bakery viewing.

restaurants.name: Il Fornaio
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian with housemade pasta and a sun-drenched plaza terrace. Popular business lunch spot.

restaurants.name: The Buena Vista Café
restaurants.description: Credited with introducing Irish Coffee to the United States in 1952. Classic American breakfasts. Always busy on weekends.

hotels.name: The Argonaut Hotel
hotels.description: Boutique hotel inside the 1907 Del Monte Cannery building. Nautical décor, Bay-view rooms, pet-friendly. A Kimpton property.

hotels.name: Hyatt Centric Fisherman's Wharf
hotels.description: 316-room hotel with 19,000+ sq ft of meeting space. Completed a sustainability-focused renovation in April 2026.

hotels.name: Hotel Bohème
hotels.description: Intimate 15-room hotel decorated in 1950s Beat-era style in the heart of North Beach.

hotels.name: San Francisco Marriott Fisherman's Wharf
hotels.description: Large full-service hotel with conference facilities, fitness center, and on-site dining. Walking distance to Pier 39.

shops.name: City Lights Booksellers & Publishers
shops.description: Legendary independent bookstore and publisher founded by Lawrence Ferlinghetti in 1953. Open daily 10 AM – 10 PM.

shops.name: Levi Strauss & Co. Campus Store
shops.description: The historic Levi's HQ campus store. Full apparel range plus SF-exclusive styles and brand archive pieces.

shops.name: Columbus Cutlery
shops.description: Neighborhood kitchen supply and knife shop since 1948. On-site knife sharpening service available.

shops.name: Ghirardelli Square
shops.description: Historic chocolate factory turned marketplace. The Ghirardelli Ice Cream & Chocolate Shop, galleries, and boutiques.

shops.name: Pier 39 Marketplace
shops.description: Open-air shopping village with 60+ specialty retailers and restaurants, plus the free K-Dock sea lion viewing area.

shops.name: Grocery Outlet (Coming Soon)
shops.description: 30,000-sq-ft grocery under construction in the former Safeway space. Opening expected 2026. First full-service grocery in the area since May 2023.
